mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate signals a robust labor market, which underpins strong consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are not positively correlated with low UNRATE (e.g., UNRATE < 4.5%) over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand, which directly impacts corporate revenues and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations are revised downwards.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines by more than 1.0% month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 10-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with a contraction in housing starts signals a tightening monetary policy that disproportionately impacts interest-sensitive sectors, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by 0.25% or more while HOUST declines by 5% or more month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances under these specific conditions.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic expansion, robust growth in industrial production reflects strong underlying economic activity and manufacturing output, boosting corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances during identified expansionary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in housing starts indicates robust construction activity and future economic growth, which translates to positive investor sentiment and S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases month-over-month by more than 2%
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained and significant growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding job market, bolstering consumer confidence and spending, which supports corporate revenues and leads to positive S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 100,000 persons month-over-month for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of triggered instances despite strong PAYEMS growth.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) erodes purchasing power and can compress corporate profit margins, leading to investor uncertainty and negative S&P 500 forward returns as real earnings decline.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases by more than 0.4% month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when inflation accelerates.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining GDP growth and rising unemployment signals a significant economic contraction or recession, leading to substantial negative S&P 500 forward returns due to widespread corporate distress and reduced earnings.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines quarter-over-quarter while UNRATE increases by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 30% of triggered instances under these severe economic conditions.
